spec.title: Focus interface: redesign decisions
spec.intro: Click the options that fit, add a note where you have one, then send it all to chat.

items.title: What controls do you want for folding rows?
items.body: Everything opens flat with no way to fold it. Which set of controls fixes that?

options: Both: a chevron on each parent plus one collapse-all / expand-all button
Chevrons only, fold each row by hand
One collapse-all / expand-all toggle, no per-row chevrons
A depth slider: show only the top N levels

items.placeholder: Note which control you'd reach for first, and any keyboard shortcut you'd want.

items.title: Opening focus on a big subtree: how should it start?
items.body: Long lists come open all at once. When focus opens on something with lots of children, what state should it land in?

options: Collapsed to the top level, you open what you need
Remember the collapse state from the main board
Remember how you left this focus last time

items.placeholder: Note what 'top level' should mean for a whole group versus a single task.

items.title: Which main-board editing powers are missing in focus?
items.body: Focus should match the main page. Check every editing power you reach for inside focus that isn't there.

options: Tab / Shift+Tab to indent and outdent
Alt+Arrow to move a row up, down, in, or out
Multi-select rows, then act on all of them

items.placeholder: Note the one whose absence stops you cold, plus anything else the board does that focus can't.

items.title: What helps you not get lost in a long focus list?
items.body: Once an outline is long, scrolling loses your place. Which navigation aid earns its keep?

options: Sticky header showing a breadcrumb of where you are
A side outline you click to jump around this list
Good collapse controls are enough, no extra nav
Both the breadcrumb and the side outline

items.placeholder: Note whether you scroll or search to find a row today.

items.title: Keep focus minimal, or move between groups and tasks inside it?
items.body: Focus hides the sidebar on purpose. Should it stay on one thing, or let you hop to the next group or task without leaving?

options: Keep it minimal, one thing at a time, exit to switch
Add prev / next to step to the sibling group or task
Add a switcher to jump to any other group or task

items.placeholder: Note if you ever wish you could jump to the next thing without exiting.

sections.heading: Where it hurts, and getting in and out

items.title: Where does focus hurt most today?
items.body: Focus runs on a single task's subtree or a whole group. Which is the bigger problem to fix first?

options: Group focus, the long many-item case
Task focus, a single task's subtree

items.placeholder: Note a real example of the one that hurts.

items.title: Entering and leaving focus: any friction?
items.body: Think about getting into focus and back out. What's the main annoyance, if any?

options: No keyboard way in or out, it's all mouse
Exiting drops me at the wrong spot on the board
Getting in takes too many clicks

items.placeholder: Note the exact moment it annoys you, going in or coming out.

items.title: Anything else about focus?
items.body: Bugs, half-formed ideas, things that feel wrong but you can't name yet. Notes only is fine.

spec.title: Update check: review the flow
spec.intro: Pick one option per state, add a note, and send it all to chat.

items.title: The whole idea
items.body: Only the downloaded copy of Punchlist checks GitHub in the background and shows a small banner when a newer build exists. Local data is never touched. The hosted copy on the website never checks.
items.placeholder: Anything about the overall flow to change before the states below.

items.title: How often it checks
items.body: The check runs in the background and never blocks the app. Open question: how often it may call GitHub.
items.placeholder: Notes on timing, throttle, or when to skip a check.

items.title: When you are already current
items.body: Most loads find nothing new. Question: stay fully silent, or show a small version stamp.

options: Silent, plus a tiny 'vX.Y.Z, current' line in Settings
Stamp in Settings and in the footer

items.placeholder: Notes on where, if anywhere, the current version shows.

items.title: Banner wording
items.body: A newer build exists. Pick the line that reads best.

options: A newer version of Punchlist is available (vX.Y.Z).
Update available. Punchlist vX.Y.Z is out.
New build vX.Y.Z. You are on vA.B.C.

items.placeholder: Reword the line however you like.

items.title: Banner buttons
items.body: The banner needs a button set. Get opens the newest build. What changed opens the release notes.

options: [Get vX.Y.Z] [What changed], X in the corner dismisses

items.placeholder: Notes on buttons, labels, or which to drop.

items.title: Banner placement
items.body: Where the banner sits when it appears. It should stay easy to ignore.

options: Thin bar pinned to the top
Thin bar pinned to the bottom
A line inside Settings only, no bar
Toast in a corner that auto-fades

items.title: What a dismissal remembers
items.body: Dismissing hides the banner. Question: when, if ever, it returns.

options: Gone until a build newer than the dismissed one appears
Back on the next app load
Back after 7 days if still not updated
Gone for good until you open Settings

items.placeholder: Notes on how sticky the dismissal should be.

items.title: When the check cannot complete
items.body: GitHub may be unreachable, error, or rate limit the check. Question: what the app does then.

options: Fail silent, show nothing, retry at the next allowed check
Fail silent, log a tiny note in Settings
Show a small 'could not check for updates' line

items.title: What leaves the machine
items.body: The only network call is one GET to api.github.com for the latest release. No personal data is sent.

options: Also mention it on the notes page
Add a toggle to turn the check off

items.placeholder: Notes on wording or where to surface it.

spec.title: Status board graph: pick a direction
spec.intro: Today's graph hides branches and crams time and tree depth onto one line. Pick the direction to fix it.

items.title: What hurts most today
items.body: The graph is a single horizontal staircase. Siblings hide behind +N pills, one axis carries both time and tree depth, and state barely shows.

options: One axis carrying both time and tree depth

items.placeholder: Anything else about today's graph that bugs you?

items.title: Layout A: local force graph (the Obsidian look)
items.body: Nodes float and links pull them into a web. Handles merges, but it jitters and lands differently each render, which is rough for a board you scan by keyboard.

options: Keep it as an optional explore toggle for later

items.placeholder: When would you actually want the organic view?

items.title: Layout B: tidy exposed tree
items.body: Root on the left, depth to the right, every sibling shown so the +N pills die. Deterministic, roughly 50 lines of vanilla, and arrow keys map to parent, child, and siblings.

options: Yes, but I want to tweak it first

items.placeholder: If you want tweaks, what would you change?

items.title: Layout C: timeline swimlanes (git-lane hybrid)
items.body: X is time. Each subagent gets a lane that opens on spawn and closes on merge, and each node fans its children into a small sub-tree. Highest ceiling for live parallel work, but the most code, and it only pays once subagents are routine.
items.sketch: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 150'><g stroke='#888' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'><line x1='20' y1='45' x2='300' y2='45'/><line x1='20' y1='105' x2='300' y2='105'/></g><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' fill='none'><line x1='30' y1='45' x2='290' y2='45'/><path d='M110 45 Q140 45 150 105'/><line x1='150' y1='105' x2='230' y2='105'/><path d='M230 105 Q252 105 260 45'/></g><g fill='currentColor'><circle cx='30' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='110' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='200' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='290' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='150' cy='105' r='5'/><circle cx='230' cy='105' r='5'/></g><g fill='#888' font-size='10'><text x='20' y='32'>main</text><text x='150' y='128'>subagent</text></g></svg>

options: Wait until subagents are common, then build it

items.placeholder: What would make subagents common enough to justify C?

items.title: What each node should encode
items.body: Beyond position, a node can carry visual signals. Pick every one you want.

options: Author badge: you vs a subagent
Focus mode dims nodes far from the active one

items.placeholder: Any encoding missing, or any you would drop?

items.title: The authoring-convention half
items.body: Half of showing state is data, not layout. Agents have to mark a node active when they start, split work into clear children, and close nodes promptly.

options: Yes, write a tightened authoring convention
No, layout changes are enough for now

items.placeholder: Which authoring habits matter most to enforce?

items.title: The recommendation
items.body: Ship Layout B now as the default so branching is fully exposed. Grow into Layout C once parallel subagents are routine. Keep Layout A as an optional toggle.

options: Go with it: ship B now, grow to C

items.placeholder: If different, what is your plan?
